1. BD Discrete-Time Survival (First Onset)

  • Data Wrangling
  • Analysis Overview
  • 1. Create Helper Functions to Use for Conducting Analyses
  • 2. Fit Discrete-Time Hazard Models
  • 3. Determine Marginal Effects: Hazard by Cluster Across Age
  • 4. Baseline Hazard (Wave Effects) - Interpretability QC
  • 5. Perform Calibration (Using Decile Bins) for the Primary Link
  • 6. Generate Inference Tables (Wald vs CR2) Primary vs Sensitivity
  • 7. Cluster Contrasts at Representative Ages (Primary Link)
  • Age Notes & Sensitivity
  • Final Story Template

Data Wrangling

First, we will read in necessary packages and use parameters to read in our pre-built analysis dataset; then filter it for complete cases (according to the necessary variables to be used in analyses) and print a brief table outlining the unique number of subjects and total observations we possess in our “complete” analysis ready dataset

Any post-onset rows remain? FALSE

Analysis Overview

Our Goal

Estimate discrete-time hazards with a logit link for first-onset bipolar outcomes-BD-I, BD-II, BD-NOS, and Any BSD-from 02A onward, using the model-ready person-period datasets built previously

Why discrete-time hazard (DTH)?

  • DTH models the probability of an onset within an interval, conditional on no prior onset. With assessments at distinct waves in ABCD, it is good practice here for us to:

    • Encode each interval as a row (person-period format)
    • Flexibly estimate the baseline hazard via wave fixed effects (i.e., dummies for 02A, 04A, 06A)
    • Include static and time-varying predictors alongside the hazard
    • This matches standard practice for event occurrence with panel waves (e.g., Singer & Willett)

  • To note, intervals with missed waves contribute longer person-periods; our cloglog + offset(log interval) sensitivity shows results are robust to irregular interval length

Why pooled logistic DTH (GLM) instead of a GLMM as primary?

  • ABCD follow-ups occur at distinct waves (02A, 04A, 06A), so event times are interval-censored by design; discrete-time hazard models on person-period data are therefore the natural framework (Singer & Willett)

  • In our person-period data, clustering is extremely sparse (1–3 rows/person) with single-event censoring, and the participant random intercept is weakly identified. In mixed-effects fits, Laplace solutions with nAGQ ≥ 1 were unstable / optimizer-dependent and frequently produced degenerate Hessians when wave indicators and within-person age were both included

  • We therefore use pooled logistic regression for primary inference and account for within-person dependence using participant-clustered robust SEs (CR2 primary; HC1 secondary). This yields stable, design-consistent inference without requiring estimation of a poorly-supported random effect structure

  • Sensitivity analyses include: (i) complementary log-log with offset(log interval length), and (ii) mixed-effects versions only when estimable/stable (reported as diagnostics/sensitivity rather than primary inference)

How we handle age (critical for interpretation)

  • Our build steps created:

    • age_mid: midpoint age for each interval
    • age_mid_cwc: within-person centered age = age_mid person_mean(age_mid)` (captures aging within individuals)
    • age_mid_between: between-person age = person_mean(age_mid) grand_mean(age_mid)` (captures older vs younger people on average)

  • Best practice in longitudinal models is to include both pieces (within & between) to avoid conflating them. This is the within-between (Mundlak/hybrid) decomposition

    • Interpretation:

      • age_mid_cwc -> change in hazard as the same person ages across waves (holding their average age constant)
      • age_mid_between -> difference in hazard for people who are on average older vs younger in this sample

    • We originally interacted cluster x age_mid_between to ask: do cluster differences widen or narrow at older average ages? We evaluated cluster x age_mid_between; it was unstable/non-significant and was dropped for parsimony and better diagnostics

In practice here, age_mid_cwc is highly collinear with the wave baseline-hazard indicators given only three intervals (VIF ~15), so our primary model includes age_mid_between plus wave indicators; we include orthogonalized within-person age (age_mid_cwc_orth) only as a sensitivity analysis too though (see below section)

Model we fit per outcome

  • Primary marginal DTH model (pooled logistic):
    • event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between
    • Inference: participant-clustered robust SEs (CR2 primary via clubSandwich; HC1 secondary via sandwich)
  • Sensitivity models:
    • Linear time: replace end_wave with wave_num (1 df)
    • Within-person age robustness: include age_mid_cwc_orth (within-person midpoint age residualized on wave indicators to avoid collinearity/identifiability issues)
    • cloglog + offset(log_dt): event ~ … with binomial(link=“cloglog”) + offset(log_dt)
    • Mixed-effects diagnostics only: glmer(… + (1|participant_id)) at nAGQ=0 to compute ICC; nAGQ=1 and allFit() used to document optimizer dependence / instability (not used for primary inference)

1. Create Helper Functions to Use for Conducting Analyses

  • build_formula_primary(): Discrete-time hazard GLM with wave fixed effects (baseline hazard), within/between age decomposition, and cluster robust effects for participant ID
  • fit_glmer_safe(): Robust GLMM fitter with bobyqa + fallback; supports logit (primary) and cloglog (sensitivity)
  • tidy_wald(): Fixed-effect ORs with Wald CIs
  • tidy_cr2(): Cluster-robust (CR2) SEs and CIs, clustered by family_id (recommended small/medium cluster correction)
  • pred_grid_by_age(): Marginal predicted first-onset hazards by cluster across chosen ages (empirical medians), fixing within-age = 0 and wave = params$wave_ref
  • calibration_df(): Decile-bin calibration (predicted vs observed)
  • auc_interval(): Interval-level ROC AUC (QC indicator; marginal)
  • diag_report(): Singularity and overdispersion checks
  • cluster_contrasts_by_age(): emmeans pairwise cluster ORs at the chosen ages

Multiple others for QC and plotting

2. Fit Discrete-Time Hazard Models

  • Iterate over BD outcomes and fit pooled logistic DTH via glm() (primary logit link)

  • Compute participant-clustered robust inference (CR2 primary; HC1 secondary)

  • Fit sensitivity models: wave_num, age_mid_cwc_orth, and cloglog + offset(log_dt)

  • Fit mixed-effects versions only as diagnostics/sensitivity: compute ICC from glmer(..., nAGQ=0) and document instability for nAGQ=1 plus optimizer dependence via allFit()

  • Persist all results (i.e., tables + model objects + diagnostic evidence) to OUT_DIR

## 2. Fit models (primary GLM + sensitivities), summarize, and save ##

#2.0 Outcomes to iterate on for this analysis
OUTCOMES <- c("bipolar_I","bipolar_II","bd_nos","any_bsd")

#2.1 Preflight: fail-fast checks to avoid multi-hour jobs dying late
preflight_dth <- function(dat, outcomes = OUTCOMES) {

  #2.1.1 Minimal required columns
  req <- c("event","cluster","sex","end_wave","age_mid_between","participant_id","family_id")
  miss <- setdiff(req, names(dat))
  if (length(miss) > 0) stop("Preflight failed: missing required columns: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))

  #2.1.2 Check factor levels exist (prevents empty levels() calls)
  if (nlevels(dat$end_wave) < 2) stop("Preflight failed: end_wave has <2 levels after filtering.")
  if (nlevels(dat$cluster) < 2)  stop("Preflight failed: cluster has <2 levels after filtering.")
  if (nlevels(dat$sex) < 1)      stop("Preflight failed: sex has 0 levels after filtering.")

  #2.1.3 Run one quick smoke test outcome to exercise:
  # - glm fit
  # - predict(newdata=...)
  # - cumrisk_by_wave_ci grid + bootMer path (with tiny B)
  out0 <- outcomes[1]
  dat0 <- dat %>% dplyr::filter(outcome == out0) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()

  #2.1.3.1 Ensure model can be fit
  fit0 <- fit_glm_safe(build_formula_primary(), dat0, link = "logit")

  #2.1.3.2 Ensure prediction helpers don't error (these are common late-fail points)
  invisible(pred_fixedwave_with_ci(fit0, dat0, ages = ages_pred_vec, wave_ref = params$wave_ref))
  invisible(hazard_by_wave_ci(fit0, dat0))
  invisible(cumrisk_by_wave_ci(fit0, dat0, B = 5))  # tiny B just to ensure code path works
  TRUE
}

message("Running preflight checks...")
Running preflight checks...
preflight_dth(bd_pp_model)

[1] TRUE

message("Preflight passed.")
Preflight passed.
#2.2 Containers
fits_primary <- list()
fits_sens_cloglog <- list()
fits_sens_linT <- list()
fits_sens_within <- list()

tbl_cr2_primary <- list()
tbl_hc1_primary <- list()

tbl_cr2_cloglog <- list()
tbl_hc1_cloglog <- list()

tbl_cr2_linT <- list()
tbl_cr2_within <- list()

tbl_cr2_primary_fam <- list()

#2.3 Evidence objects supporting why GLMM is not primary for this analysis
vif_evidence <- list()
icc_evidence <- list()
allfit_evidence <- list()
or_compare <- list()

#2.3.1 Model decision log; seed with previously-attempted families so they appear in the HTML
model_log <- decision_log_init()

model_log <- add_decision(
  model_log,
  model_family = "DTH pooled logistic (primary)",
  engine = "glm + clubSandwich",
  specification = "event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between; CR2 clustered by participant (HC1 secondary)",
  status = "used_primary",
  key_diagnostics = "Stable fits across outcomes; design-consistent inference with clustered SEs",
  decision_rationale = "Primary model: avoids weakly-identified random intercept with 1–3 rows/person and sparse events")

model_log <- add_decision(
  model_log,
  model_family = "DTH cloglog + offset(log_dt)",
  engine = "glm + clubSandwich",
  specification = "event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between + offset(log_dt); CR2 clustered by participant",
  status = "sensitivity",
  key_diagnostics = "Robustness to unequal interval lengths (missed waves -> longer dt)",
  decision_rationale = "Sensitivity: approximates continuous-time proportional hazards under discrete intervals")

#2.3.2 Seed previously tried approaches even if not run in this script
model_log <- add_decision(
  model_log,
  model_family = "GLMM (random intercept)",
  engine = "lme4::glmer",
  specification = "event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between + (1|participant_id) [+ optional (1|family_id)/(1|site_factor)]",
  status = "diagnostic_only",
  key_diagnostics = "Often optimizer-dependent / degenerate Hessians when wave FE + within-age included; RE weakly identified",
  decision_rationale = "Documented as diagnostics; not used for primary inference")

model_log <- add_decision(
  model_log,
  model_family = "GAMM / spline time (previous)",
  engine = "mgcv (e.g., gam/bam)",
  specification = "event ~ cluster + sex + s(age_mid) + (random effects as applicable) + wave terms",
  status = "attempted_previous",
  key_diagnostics = "Add your prior notes/results here (e.g., concurvity, unstable smooths with only 3 waves, separation in cells)",
  decision_rationale = "Not carried forward: limited unique timepoints (3 waves) makes smooth terms poorly supported")

model_log <- add_decision(
  model_log,
  model_family = "glmmTMB (previous)",
  engine = "glmmTMB",
  specification = "Binomial mixed model variants (including alternative RE structures / dispersion handling)",
  status = "attempted_previous",
  key_diagnostics = "Add your prior notes/results here (e.g., convergence warnings, boundary fits, separation)",
  decision_rationale = "Not carried forward if convergence/identifiability issues persisted or estimates were optimizer-dependent")

model_log <- add_decision(
  model_log,
  model_family = "Bayesian (brms) (previous)",
  engine = "brms",
  specification = "Bernoulli/logit or cloglog with priors; random intercepts; optional varying effects",
  status = "attempted_previous",
  key_diagnostics = "Add your prior notes/results here (e.g., divergent transitions, weak ID for RE, sensitivity to priors)",
  decision_rationale = "Not carried forward if computationally heavy without improving stability/interpretability given sparse intervals")

#2.4 Loop per outcome
for (out in OUTCOMES) {

  message("Primary GLM (logit): ", out)
  dat_o <- bd_pp_model %>% dplyr::filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  
  #2.4.0 Guard: ensure sex has at least 1 non-NA level and remains a factor
  if (!("sex" %in% names(dat_o))) stop("dat_o missing sex for outcome ", out)
  if (!is.factor(dat_o$sex)) dat_o$sex <- factor(dat_o$sex)
  if (nlevels(dat_o$sex) < 1) stop("sex has 0 levels for outcome ", out)

  #2.4.1 Primary pooled logistic DTH
  f_primary <- build_formula_primary()
  m_glm <- fit_glm_safe(f_primary, dat_o, link = "logit")
  fits_primary[[out]] <- m_glm
  
  #2.4.2 GLM diagnostics: separation, influence, binned residuals, check_model panels
  #2.4.2.1 Separation screen for the actual primary model too
  sep_primary <- sep_screen(f_primary, dat_o, family = binomial(link="logit"))
  write_if_any(sep_primary, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue("sep_primary_{out}.csv")))
  
  #2.4.2.2 Append outcome-specific diagnostic snapshot to the model decision log
  nevents <- sum(dat_o$event == 1L, na.rm = TRUE)
  nrows <- nrow(dat_o)
  sep_flag <- if ("separation" %in% names(sep_primary)) as.character(sep_primary$separation[1]) else NA_character_

  model_log <- add_decision(
    model_log,
    model_family = "DTH pooled logistic (primary)",
    engine = "glm + clubSandwich",
    specification = "event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between; CR2 clustered by participant",
    outcome = out,
    status = "used_primary",
    key_diagnostics = glue::glue("n_rows={nrows}; n_events={nevents}; separation={sep_flag}"),
    decision_rationale = "Primary inference run")
  
  #2.4.2.3 Sparse cell scan; helps interpret separation / instability
  sc <- sparse_cells(dat_o, vars = c("end_wave","cluster","sex"), y = "event", min_n = 5)
  write_if_any(sc, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue("sparse_cells_{out}.csv")))
  
  #2.4.2.4 Influence measures
  infl_tbl <- tibble(
    outcome = out,
    .row = seq_len(nrow(dat_o)),
    hat = hatvalues(m_glm),
    cooksD = cooks.distance(m_glm))
  write_if_any(infl_tbl, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue("influence_basic_{out}.csv")))
  
  #2.4.2.5 Top influential rows
  top_infl <- infl_tbl %>% arrange(desc(cooksD)) %>% slice(1:25)
  write_if_any(top_infl, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue("influence_top25_{out}.csv")))
  
  #2.4.2.6 Binned residual plot (arm::binnedplot if available; otherwise skip)
  if (requireNamespace("arm", quietly = TRUE)) {
    fn <- file.path(OUT_DIR, glue("binned_resid_{out}.png"))
    if (requireNamespace("ragg", quietly = TRUE)) ragg::agg_png(fn, width=1800, height=1200, res=144) else png(fn, width=1800, height=1200, res=144)
    arm::binnedplot(x = fitted(m_glm), y = residuals(m_glm, type = "response"),
                    xlab = "Fitted", ylab = "Response residual")
    dev.off()
  }
  
  #2.4.2.7 check_model panels to file
  save_check_model_plots(m_glm, tag = glue("glm_{out}"))

  #2.4.3 Participant-clustered inference (CR2 primary; HC1 secondary)
  tbl_cr2_primary[[out]] <- safe_or_null(
  tidy_glm_cr2(m_glm, cluster_var = dat_o$participant_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(outcome = out, model = "logit_primary", se_type = "CR2", .before = 1))

  tbl_hc1_primary[[out]] <- safe_or_null(
  tidy_glm_hc1(m_glm, cluster_var = dat_o$participant_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(outcome = out, model = "logit_primary", se_type = "HC1", .before = 1))
  
  tbl_cr2_primary_fam[[out]] <- tidy_glm_cr2(m_glm, cluster_var = dat_o$family_id) %>%
  mutate(outcome = out, model = "logit_primary", se_type = "CR2_family", .before = 1)

  #2.4.4 Sensitivity A: linear time (wave_num)
  m_linT <- fit_glm_safe(build_formula_lin_time(), dat_o, link = "logit")
  fits_sens_linT[[out]] <- m_linT
  tbl_cr2_linT[[out]] <- tidy_glm_cr2(m_linT, cluster_var = dat_o$participant_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(outcome = out, model = "logit_lin_time", se_type = "CR2", .before = 1)

  #2.4.5 Sensitivity B: orthogonalized within-person age
  m_within <- fit_glm_safe(build_formula_within_orth(), dat_o, link = "logit")
  fits_sens_within[[out]] <- m_within
  tbl_cr2_within[[out]] <- tidy_glm_cr2(m_within, cluster_var = dat_o$participant_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(outcome = out, model = "logit_within_orth", se_type = "CR2", .before = 1)

  #2.4.6 Sensitivity C: cloglog + offset(log_dt)
  f_cloglog <- update.formula(f_primary, . ~ . + offset(log_dt))
  sep_cloglog <- sep_screen(f_cloglog, dat_o, family = binomial(link="cloglog"))
  m_clog <- fit_glm_safe(f_cloglog, dat_o, link = "cloglog")
  fits_sens_cloglog[[out]] <- m_clog

  tbl_cr2_cloglog[[out]] <- tidy_glm_cr2(m_clog, cluster_var = dat_o$participant_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(outcome = out, model = "cloglog_offset", se_type = "CR2", .before = 1)

  tbl_hc1_cloglog[[out]] <- tidy_glm_hc1(m_clog, cluster_var = dat_o$participant_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(outcome = out, model = "cloglog_offset", se_type = "HC1", .before = 1)

  #2.4.7 Evidence supporting dropping raw age_mid_cwc from primary when using wave indicators, AKA VIF / collinearity
  vif_tbl <- safe_or_null({
    cc <- performance::check_collinearity(glm(event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between + age_mid_cwc,
                                              data = dat_o, family = binomial()))
    tibble::as_tibble(cc) %>% dplyr::mutate(outcome = out, .before = 1)
  })
  vif_evidence[[out]] <- vif_tbl

  #2.4.8 Evidence: GLMM ICC + allFit optimizer dependence at nAGQ>=1 (diagnostic only)
  #2.4.8.1 ICC from nAGQ=0 participant-only RE
  glmm0 <- safe_or_null(
    lme4::glmer(event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between + (1|participant_id),
                data = dat_o, family = binomial(), nAGQ = 0,
                control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl=list(maxfun=2e5)))
  )
  if (!is.null(glmm0)) {
    vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(glmm0))$vcov[1]
    icc <- vc / (vc + (pi^2 / 3))
    icc_evidence[[out]] <- tibble::tibble(outcome = out, vcov = vc, icc = icc)
  }

  #2.4.8.2 nAGQ=1 + allFit: store convergence/degeneracy across optimizers if it runs
  glmm1 <- safe_or_null(tryCatch(update(glmm0, nAGQ = 1), error = function(e) NULL))
  if (!is.null(glmm1)) {
    af <- safe_or_null(lme4::allFit(glmm1))
    if (!is.null(af)) {
      ss <- summary(af)
      allfit_evidence[[out]] <- tibble::tibble(
        outcome = out,
        n_ok = ss$OK,
        n_fail = ss$failed,
        n_warn = ss$warnings
      )
      saveRDS(af, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue::glue("allFit_nAGQ1_{out}.rds")))
    }
  }

  #2.4.9 Evidence of point-estimate stability in GLM vs glmer nAGQ=0
  if (!is.null(glmm0)) {
    or_compare[[out]] <- tibble::tibble(
      outcome = out,
      OR_glm = exp(coef(m_glm)[["clusterC2"]]),
      OR_glmer_nAGQ0 = exp(lme4::fixef(glmm0)[["clusterC2"]])
    )
    saveRDS(glmm0, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue::glue("glmer_nAGQ0_diag_{out}.rds")))
  }
}
Primary GLM (logit): bipolar_I
Primary GLM (logit): bipolar_II
Primary GLM (logit): bd_nos

bobyqa : [OK] Nelder_Mead : [failed] nlminbwrap :

boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

[OK] nmkbw : [failed] optimx.L-BFGS-B : [failed] nloptwrap.NLOPT_LN_NELDERMEAD : [failed] nloptwrap.NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA : [OK]

Primary GLM (logit): any_bsd
#2.5 Bind and save outputs
cr2_all <- bind_rows(compact(c(tbl_cr2_primary, tbl_cr2_primary_fam,
                               tbl_cr2_linT, tbl_cr2_within, tbl_cr2_cloglog)))
hc1_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::compact(c(tbl_hc1_primary, tbl_hc1_cloglog)))

vif_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::compact(vif_evidence))
icc_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::compact(icc_evidence))
allfit_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::compact(allfit_evidence))
or_compare_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::compact(or_compare))

readr::write_csv(cr2_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_inference_CR2.csv"))
readr::write_csv(hc1_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_inference_HC1_participant.csv"))
write_if_any(vif_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_vif_evidence.csv"))
write_if_any(icc_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_icc_evidence.csv"))
write_if_any(allfit_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_allFit_nAGQ1_summary.csv"))
write_if_any(or_compare_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_or_compare_glm_vs_glmer0.csv"))

#2.5.1 Persist and display the model decision log
readr::write_csv(model_log, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_model_decision_log.csv"))

knitr::kable(
  model_log,
  caption = "Model decision log (primary + sensitivity + previously attempted strategies)") %>% kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed","responsive"))
Model decision log (primary + sensitivity + previously attempted strategies)
model_family engine specification outcome status key_diagnostics decision_rationale artifact
DTH pooled logistic (primary) glm + clubSandwich event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between; CR2 clustered by participant (HC1 secondary) NA used_primary Stable fits across outcomes; design-consistent inference with clustered SEs Primary model: avoids weakly-identified random intercept with 1–3 rows/person and sparse events NA
DTH cloglog + offset(log_dt) glm + clubSandwich event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between + offset(log_dt); CR2 clustered by participant NA sensitivity Robustness to unequal interval lengths (missed waves -> longer dt) Sensitivity: approximates continuous-time proportional hazards under discrete intervals NA
GLMM (random intercept) lme4::glmer event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between + (1&#124;participant_id) [+ optional (1&#124;family_id)/(1&#124;site_factor)] NA diagnostic_only Often optimizer-dependent / degenerate Hessians when wave FE + within-age included; RE weakly identified Documented as diagnostics; not used for primary inference NA
GAMM / spline time (previous) mgcv (e.g., gam/bam) event ~ cluster + sex + s(age_mid) + (random effects as applicable) + wave terms NA attempted_previous Add your prior notes/results here (e.g., concurvity, unstable smooths with only 3 waves, separation in cells) Not carried forward: limited unique timepoints (3 waves) makes smooth terms poorly supported NA
glmmTMB (previous) glmmTMB Binomial mixed model variants (including alternative RE structures / dispersion handling) NA attempted_previous Add your prior notes/results here (e.g., convergence warnings, boundary fits, separation) Not carried forward if convergence/identifiability issues persisted or estimates were optimizer-dependent NA
Bayesian (brms) (previous) brms Bernoulli/logit or cloglog with priors; random intercepts; optional varying effects NA attempted_previous Add your prior notes/results here (e.g., divergent transitions, weak ID for RE, sensitivity to priors) Not carried forward if computationally heavy without improving stability/interpretability given sparse intervals NA
DTH pooled logistic (primary) glm + clubSandwich event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between; CR2 clustered by participant bipolar_I used_primary n_rows=19516; n_events=198; separation=NA Primary inference run NA
DTH pooled logistic (primary) glm + clubSandwich event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between; CR2 clustered by participant bipolar_II used_primary n_rows=19572; n_events=78; separation=NA Primary inference run NA
DTH pooled logistic (primary) glm + clubSandwich event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between; CR2 clustered by participant bd_nos used_primary n_rows=19535; n_events=204; separation=NA Primary inference run NA
DTH pooled logistic (primary) glm + clubSandwich event ~ cluster + sex + end_wave + age_mid_between; CR2 clustered by participant any_bsd used_primary n_rows=19418; n_events=431; separation=NA Primary inference run NA 
#2.6 Create pred objects for downstream utilization
#2.6.1 Wald tables for reference from model-based SE; not robust
wald_all <- bind_rows(lapply(fits_primary, tidy_wald), .id = "outcome") %>%
  mutate(model = "logit_primary", .before = 1)

#2.6.2 Predictions for plots using primary link
pred_all_fixedwave <- bind_rows(lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  df  <- pred_fixedwave_with_ci(fit, dat, ages = ages_pred_vec, wave_ref = params$wave_ref)
  mutate(df, outcome = out, model = params$link_primary, pred_mode = "fixed_wave", .before = 1)
}))

pred_all_aligned <- bind_rows(lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  df  <- pred_aligned_with_ci(fit, dat, ages = ages_pred_vec)
  mutate(df, outcome = out, model = params$link_primary, pred_mode = "wave_aligned", .before = 1)
}))

#2.6.3 Calibration deciles using primary link
cal_all <- bind_rows(lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  df <- calibration_df(fit, dat)
  mutate(df, outcome = out, model = params$link_primary, .before = 1)
}))

#2.6.4 Hazard-by-wave tables
hazard_tables_all <- bind_rows(lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  df  <- hazard_by_wave_ci(fit, dat)
  mutate(df, outcome = out, model = params$link_primary, .before = 1)
}))

#2.6.5 Cumulative risk tables
cumrisk_tables_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  df <- tryCatch(
    cumrisk_by_wave_ci(fit, dat, B = 500),
    error = function(e) {
      warning("cumrisk_by_wave_ci failed for ", out, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
      tidyr::crossing(
        end_wave = factor(levels(dat$end_wave), levels = levels(dat$end_wave)),
        cluster = factor(levels(dat$cluster), levels = levels(dat$cluster))) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(
          cumrisk = NA_real_,
          cumrisk_lo = NA_real_,
          cumrisk_hi = NA_real_)
    })

  dplyr::mutate(df, outcome = out, model = params$link_primary, .before = 1)
}))

#2.6.6 Cluster contrasts
emm_all <- bind_rows(lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  df  <- cluster_contrasts_by_age(fit, dat, ages = ages_pred_vec, wave_ref = params$wave_ref)
  mutate(df, outcome = out, model = params$link_primary, .before = 1)
}))

emm_wave_primary_all <- bind_rows(lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out) %>% droplevels() %>% prep_dth_terms()
  df  <- cluster_contrasts_by_wave_aligned(fit, dat)
  mutate(df, outcome = out, model = params$link_primary, .before = 1)
}))

#2.6.7 Write all pred objects to disk for reference
write_csv(wald_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_wald_reference.csv"))
write_csv(pred_all_fixedwave, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_preds_fixedwave.csv"))
write_csv(pred_all_aligned, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_preds_wave_aligned.csv"))
write_csv(cal_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_calibration_deciles.csv"))
write_csv(hazard_tables_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_hazard_by_wave.csv"))
write_csv(cumrisk_tables_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_cumrisk_by_wave.csv"))
write_csv(emm_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_emmeans_contrasts_by_age.csv"))
write_csv(emm_wave_primary_all, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bd_dth_emmeans_contrasts_by_wave.csv"))

#2.7 Save models
purrr::iwalk(fits_primary, ~ saveRDS(.x, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue::glue("glm_primary_{.y}.rds"))))
purrr::iwalk(fits_sens_linT, ~ saveRDS(.x, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue::glue("glm_linT_{.y}.rds"))))
purrr::iwalk(fits_sens_within, ~ saveRDS(.x, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue::glue("glm_withinOrth_{.y}.rds"))))
purrr::iwalk(fits_sens_cloglog, ~ saveRDS(.x, file.path(OUT_DIR, glue::glue("glm_cloglogOffset_{.y}.rds"))))

#2.8 Show key evidence tables in HTML
if (nrow(icc_all) > 0) {
  knitr::kable(icc_all, digits = 4, caption = "ICC evidence from participant-only glmer (nAGQ=0; diagnostic)") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed","responsive"))
}
ICC evidence from participant-only glmer (nAGQ=0; diagnostic)
outcome vcov icc
bipolar_I 0.1182 0.0347
bipolar_II 0.2841 0.0795
bd_nos 0.1088 0.0320
any_bsd 0.1561 0.0453 
if (nrow(or_compare_all) > 0) {
  knitr::kable(or_compare_all, digits = 4, caption = "Point-estimate stability: OR(cluster) GLM vs glmer nAGQ=0") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed","responsive"))
}
Point-estimate stability: OR(cluster) GLM vs glmer nAGQ=0
outcome OR_glm OR_glmer_nAGQ0
bipolar_I 2.9919 2.9975
bipolar_II 2.2685 2.2774
bd_nos 3.3628 3.3690
any_bsd 2.7435 2.7590 
if (nrow(allfit_all) > 0) {
  knitr::kable(allfit_all, digits = 0, caption = "Optimizer dependence: allFit summary for nAGQ=1 fits (diagnostic)") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed","responsive"))
}
Optimizer dependence: allFit summary for nAGQ=1 fits (diagnostic)
outcome
bd_nos

3. Determine Marginal Effects: Hazard by Cluster Across Age

Generates visualizations of predicted first-onset hazard vs age for each outcome and cluster, pairing each empirical age with its corresponding wave (02A/04A/06A) and within-person age deviation at 0. These visualize risk separation and trajectories across the median ages in the data at each timepoint

How to read the hazard plots (interpretation guide)

  • Fixed-wave age curves (first panel): We hold the wave constant at ses-04A and vary age.
    This isolates how the hazard changes with age within a given wave. Lines show model-predicted first-onset hazard per interval; higher lines = higher risk. Compare clusters at the same age to see separation

  • Wave-aligned curves (second panel): Each point/age is paired with its actual wave (e.g., median ages at 02A, 04A, 06A).
    This shows how the baseline hazard changes across waves while also reflecting the typical age at each wave.
    A downward slope here does not contradict rising age risk - it reflects how the wave baseline (calendar-time hazard) moves across assessments

  • QC overlay (model vs empirical): Hollow points are empirical per-wave hazards; solid lines are the model predictions on the same x-axis (wave). Close agreement indicates good calibration by wave

  • Cumulative first-onset risk (third panel): Converts per-interval hazards into the probability of having had a first onset by each wave

    • This is the “when do onsets happen” view most would expect - rising curves that separate by cluster indicate earlier and/or more frequent onsets in the higher-risk cluster

4. Baseline Hazard (Wave Effects) - Interpretability QC

Uses the fitted model to compute marginal baseline hazards by wave (sessions 02A, 04A, 06A) while holding other covariates at reference. Bar plots provide a quick sanity check that interval risks align with expected cohort timing

## 4. Baseline hazard (wave FE -> marginal hazard by wave) ##

#4.1 Create a helper function to plot marginal hazard effects by assessment timepoint (wave)
plot_wave_hazard <- function(fit, data, title) {
  nd <- expand.grid(end_wave = levels(data$end_wave), cluster = levels(data$cluster)[1], age_mid_between = 0, age_mid_cwc = 0, sex = levels(data$sex)[1])
  nd$pred <- predict(fit, newdata = nd, type = "response", re.form = NA)
  ggplot(nd, aes(x = end_wave, y = pred)) +
    geom_col() +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.001)) +
    labs(title = wrap_title(title, 70), x = "Wave (interval end)", y = "Baseline hazard (marginal)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(lineheight = 0.95))
}

#4.2 Use the helper function to generate marginal hazard effects by wave plots
wave_plots <- lapply(OUTCOMES, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>% filter(outcome == out)
  plot_wave_hazard(fit, dat, glue("Baseline (wave) hazard - {out} (link={params$link_primary})"))
})

#4.3 Facet wrap the plots
wrap_plots(wave_plots, ncol = 2)

5. Perform Calibration (Using Decile Bins) for the Primary Link

Creates decile-bin calibration plots (mean predicted vs observed event rate). This checks probability calibration of the discrete-time models and helps flag miscalculation across the risk spectrum

6. Generate Inference Tables (Wald vs CR2) Primary vs Sensitivity

We report participant-clustered robust inference as primary (CR2; HC1 secondary). Model-based (Wald) SEs from the pooled logistic model are shown only as a reference, since they assume independent rows and can be anti-conservative when within-person clustering is present

To note, odds ratios are per-interval hazard ratios on the logit scale; cluster main effects represent the average cluster gap across the modeled age range unless a clusterxage interaction is present (in which case interpret the interaction at the reported ages)

7. Cluster Contrasts at Representative Ages (Primary Link)

Reports emmeans pairwise cluster contrasts (odds-ratio scale) at wave_ref with within-person age = 0 and baseline/sex/site at reference. This isolates how cluster differences manifest at clinically interpretable age points

Age Notes & Sensitivity

Why decompose age?

  • In longitudinal hazards, naive “age” conflates two distinct things:
    • Aging within the same youth (time passing)
    • Older vs younger youths on average (between families/ids)
  • Including age_mid_cwc + age_mid_between separates these. Interpretation:
    • age_mid_cwc: per-person change in hazard as they age (holding their own mean age fixed)
    • age_mid_between: difference in hazard for a child whose typical age is higher vs lower than the cohort mean

An optional parsimonious alternative would be to use a single centered age. If we end up needing a simpler reporting model, we could fit: event ~ cluster * age_mid_gmc + baseline_status + sex + site_factor + end_wave + (1|id) + (1|family); and keep the decomposed model as a sensitivity analysis (this is currently not run here though, to keep runtime minimal)

Final Story Template

In the interest of summarizing the many and complex analyses that were ran in service of answering the “when” of BD onset by risk cluster, the following is an attempt to summarize the most pertinent and meaningful analysis results.
